sampling_note: Two replays per variant: baseline=[1,14], rush=[27,41], turtle=[58,73], flank=[86,99]. Mix of wins (1, 27, 58, 73, 99) and timeouts (14, 41, 86) selected per the request to cover both outcomes.

per_replay_scores.notes: Win at T10. Canonical script: soap-dish T2, towel-rack T4, wall-crack T5, ceiling at T6, three-way ceiling fight T8 (pathfinders volley x3 deletes elites u0030-32 then loses to spider-queen; vanguard-bravo wins second engagement; silk-line crashes vanguard-alpha and dies clean). spider-web captured T10 by vanguard-bravo punching through with potato-bug u0020 doing 1 dmg/round. Reads coherently for 8 turns; battle outcomes feel earned because pathfinders softens the elites first. Queen ultimate charge climbs to 55 then game ends — never fires.

Timeout at T100. Tick-for-tick identical to seed 1 through T8: same captures, same volleys, same casualties. Diverges only at T10 second push to web-guard — vanguard-bravo loses this time (one extra footman casualty in the prior attrition) and retreats. From T11 onward: web-watch wanders to (8,0)→north-wall, then no further events for 89 turns except queen-ultimate-charged. Queen ult reaches 100 at T19, logged as charge=100 81 more times. Outcome-credibility 0 — the ants did the same thing as their winning seed and the dice rolled differently inside one battle, with no recovery mechanism.

Win at T8 — fastest in the sample. Pathfinders climb-bypass to ceiling at T0 (1,1)→ceiling, walk the ceiling diagonal to (9,9) by T5, attack web-guard with three volleys; vanguard-bravo follows up and finishes spider-queen u0029 at T7. Genuinely satisfying decision arc: rush trades soap-dish/towel-rack control for tempo and pulls it off. The volley-then-mend prelude reads like deliberate setup. Highest-watchability replay in the sample.

Timeout. Same opening as seed 27, but T4 vanguard-bravo collides with spider 'advance-scout' (u0045-48) at ceiling (7,8) and wins a costly battle (u0017 footman killed). T5 pathfinders attack web-guard and lose the canonical pathfinders-vs-queen attrition; vanguard-bravo retreats. T6 both parties try web-watch with no leader and get wiped (single round each). From T7 onward all field parties dead, queen ult charges to cap, no scenario-end. Loss is credible-ish (rush met an extra defender) but the inability to recover — no reinforcements ever leave home base — is not.

Win at T22. Vanguard-alpha solos the floor capture chain (soap-dish T2, towel-rack T4, wall-crack T5) and parries silk-line at north-wall (8,5) on T9 with a clean wipe (zero ant casualties — the strongest single-party fight in the sample). Pathfinders + vanguard-bravo sit idle at storm-drain through T15 then begin a 5-turn march to ceiling (9,9) for the canonical T21 web-guard double-push. Tactical-variety bumped because the silk-line counter-push is genuinely interesting, but the assault itself is bit-identical to baseline T8.

Win at T21. Vanguard-alpha takes a slightly different floor route (via x=2,y=0) but still hits the same POSTs at T2/T4/T5. Silk-line marches through ceiling unopposed and parks at north-wall (8,0) — never engages because the turtle's vanguard-alpha never enters the kill zone. Pathfinders/vanguard-bravo wake at T16 and execute the canonical double-push at T21. Outcome credible (long siege earns the win) but turns 9-15 are 7 consecutive turn-starts with only queen-ultimate-charged events — pure dead air.

Timeout. Tick-for-tick identical to baseline-1 through T6 (same captures at the same ticks; flank does NOT actually flank). At T7, pathfinders route via (8,7)→(8,8)→(8,9) and engage web-watch (u0034-39, six soldiers/scouts) instead of web-guard; volley deletes 3 spider-soldiers, pathfinders win the fight but lose mage u0023 and archer u0027 in a 3-round battle. T8 surviving pathfinders push to (9,9) into web-guard at full strength and get wiped in ONE round (queen + elites alive); vanguard-bravo loses next round despite landing one elite kill. From T10 onward, silk-line idles at north-wall (9,5), no further events for 89 turns. Outcome 0 — reads like the engine ate the assault.

Win at T9. Same first-7-turn trajectory as baseline-1 (flank's micro-route through (5,3)→(6,3) instead of (5,5)→(5,4) is purely cosmetic). T8 pathfinders-vs-web-guard is the canonical loss; T9 vanguard-bravo wins the second engagement. Differs from baseline-1 only by one tick of pathfinder positioning. flank and baseline are functionally indistinguishable.

findings.observation: Every web-guard battle in 8/8 sampled replays plays out identically because all damage is floored at 1. Spider-queen u0029 has armor 3; ant-archer atk 4, ant-footman atk 5, ant-mage atk 4, ant-potato-bug atk 4-ish — every attacker rolls atk-3 and lands the floor-1 damage. Sample evidence: in baseline-1 round-by-round, archer u0021, mage u0022, footman u0017/u0018/u0019, and potato-bug u0020 ALL deal 'damage:1, killed:false' against u0029-spider-queen — see ticks 121-122 (vanguard-bravo final assault). The supposedly 'heavy melee' potato-bug deals exactly the same 1 dmg as a footman or archer. Volley fires three times per assault and always kills exactly elites u0030-u0032 (never u0033, never the queen) because volley damage cleanly exceeds elite armor but cannot one-shot the queen. Net consequence: the only composition that wins web-guard is 'maximize body count', which is what vanguard-bravo (6 bodies) has and pathfinders (5 archery-tilted bodies after volley pre-kills) lacks. Pathfinders LOSE web-guard in 8/8 sampled web-guard fights; vanguard-bravo WINS web-guard in 6/8 cases (the two losses are flank-86 and rush-41 where they engaged depleted). Heavy-melee, archer-heavy, and mage-heavy are not viable — only 'most warm bodies' is.
findings.suggested_action: Tune troop mechanics (lever permitted): drop spider-queen armor from 3 to 2 (or queen HP from ~36 to ~28) so that potato-bug atk creates a real damage step (e.g., potato-bug deals 2 vs queen, footman deals 3, archer deals 1) and heavy-melee comps become genuinely stronger than archer comps against the queen. Alternatively, give potato-bug an 'armor-piercing' or 'big-attack' tag that bypasses 2 points of armor. Either change creates a strict ordering among ant unit types vs queen, which is what makes composition matter.

findings.observation: Although summary.json shows 4/4 variants pass the 40% win-rate floor, only 2 of the 4 variants are actually distinct strategies. Baseline (seeds 1, 14) and flank (seeds 86, 99) produce tick-for-tick identical event streams through T6 and differ only in cosmetic pathfinder y-coordinate ordering on T3 — same captures at ticks 33/56/69, same battles at the same ticks, same casualties. avgEventsPerRun=193.99 (baseline) vs 191.04 (flank) and avgTurnsToVictory=10.06 vs 10.06 confirm the duplication. The 'flank' AI is not a different strategy, it is a renamed baseline. Turtle (58, 73) is also baseline-with-a-delay: the assault path, battle composition, casualties, and exact attack ticks at T21 match baseline T8 — turtle just adds 13 idle turns. Only rush (27, 41) does something genuinely different (climb-bypass, ground POSTs unowned, fastest win). Spec demands 'three meaningfully different strategic approaches'; we have two (rush, push-up-the-ladder).
findings.suggested_action: Lever 2 (initial circumstances): change flank's starting party positions to force a wall-plane entry from the OPPOSITE side (e.g., put one flank party near a south-wall or east-wall plane transition) so flank routes do not converge on towel-rack/wall-crack. Alternatively, lever 1 (spider AI): give web-watch a counter-push toward wall-crack triggered when wall-crack flips ant — that punishes the canonical baseline path and rewards flank parties that approach from the opposite wall, creating a genuine route choice.

findings.observation: Three of four variants land at 64-66% — baseline 64.0%, flank 64.0%, rush 66.0%, turtle 66.0%. Per the rubric the band is [65%, 80%]; baseline and flank sit one point below the floor. The dominant timeout failure mode is the same in all four variants: pathfinders dies first in the web-guard fight, vanguard-bravo follows up but loses a coin-flip on whether the queen kills 4 vs 5 ant bodies before being attrited. baseline-14 vs baseline-1 differ only inside one battle's damage rolls. 36 timeouts in baseline alone suggests the win rate is brittle around the floor.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 2 (initial circumstances): add one extra archer or footman to vanguard-bravo (cost: one slot reshuffle in roster-ants.json) so the web-guard double-push has a one-body buffer against the dice. This is the smallest tuning change that should pull baseline/flank from 64% to ~70% without overshooting rush/turtle past 80%.

findings.observation: Volley and mend abilities fire (good — improvement over the prior fun.json which scored 0). However the AI uses them as a fixed prologue: every web-guard engagement begins with 'pathfinders volley x3, mend, then attack'. There is zero situational use — no volley saved for web-watch in flank-86 (where it would have won the second fight), no mend used to keep a key unit alive in rush-41. Royal Jelly never fires. Queen ultimate never fires (charge maxes at 100 and stays there for 70+ turns in every timeout sampled). The texture is: scripted volley-prelude → scripted attrition battle → win or stalemate.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 1 (enemy pattern) AND lever 4 (scenario goal) jointly: (a) give spider-queen an action that triggers when her HP drops below 50% (e.g., a single AoE 'web-burst' that clears one ant unit per fight) so reserved-volley/mend become defensible decisions rather than always-prologue. (b) add a scenario-end trigger 'spider-queen u0029 dies' that fires queen-ultimate as the ant queen's celebratory finisher when ant queen ult >= 80 — this gives the charge-100 log line a destination and makes the back half of timeouts watchable.

findings.observation: Wins are credible: rush-27 reads as a genuine speed strategy; turtle-58/73 read as patient sieges; baseline-1 reads as a coordinated multi-party assault. Losses are NOT credible. baseline-14, flank-86, and rush-41 all end with the entire field force dead and the home base party (vanguard-alpha or queen-guard) alive but unable to redeploy — there is no 'second wave' mechanism. The replay just stops generating events for 80-90 turns. To a reader this looks like the engine forgot the ants exist. This is an improvement over the prior fun.json (zero wins then) but the failure mode is still inarticulate.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 4 (scenario goal): add a supplementary win condition 'hold 4 of 5 POSTs for 8 consecutive turns' so timeouts where ants own 4 POSTs (every timeout sampled has antPostsAtEnd=4 for baseline/flank/turtle) resolve to ant victory. This pulls win rate up AND makes the late game articulable. Already proposed in prior fun.json finding; reiterating because still not adopted.

findings.observation: Major improvement over the prior fun-rubric run (was 0/3, now 1/3): rush replays are genuinely interesting (different start, fast finish), turtle adds a credible silk-line counter-engagement (seed 58 north-wall battle is the best single fight in the sample), and the 6-plane geometry adds visible north-wall hops that read as 'climbing the wall' rather than teleporting. However, the 8-replay batch is still hurt by (a) baseline ≈ flank duplication, (b) every web-guard fight running on rails, (c) timeouts producing 80+ turns of pure queen-ultimate-charged spam. The 6-plane geometry helped but did not fix the underlying scriptedness.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 1 (spider AI): once ants own ≥3 POSTs, send web-watch on a counter-push down north-wall toward wall-crack, so the ceiling assault has to fight on TWO fronts. This consumes the dead air in both timeouts and successful runs, and gives flank/baseline a route distinction (flank's western approach would dodge the counter-push).

findings.observation: Baseline scores 2/3 here rather than 0/3 (huge improvement on prior). Rush genuinely uses the climb-bypass and is route-distinct from the others. The 6-plane geometry's north-wall hop adds visible plane-transition events that did not exist before. The remaining gap is that turtle and flank do not exercise route-distinct geography — they reuse the baseline ladder.
findings.suggested_action: Lever 3 (battlefield): add a second plane-transition pair on the opposite side of the bathroom (e.g., a 'shower-curtain' POST pair on east-wall ↔ ceiling at x=0) so flank parties have a geometrically distinct path to the spider-web. This is a battlefield change, not an AI change, and gives flank a real reason to route differently from baseline.
